name: Parmesan Asparagus Spears
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir together the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, and Dijon mustard.
2. Set aside while cooking the asparagus.
3. Heat 2 inches of water and half the salt to boiling in a large, deep skillet.
4. Break or slice off the tough lower stems of the asparagus spears.
5. Rinse and place half the spears in the boiling water. Cover and cook for 5 to 6 minutes, or until tender crisp.
6. Transfer the asparagus with tongs or a slotted spatula to the bowl with the vinegar mixture.
7. In fresh boiling water, cook the remaining asparagus with the rest of the salt.
8. Transfer to the bowl with the vinegar mixture.
9. Toss gently until the spears are evenly coated.
10. Place the asparagus on a serving plate. Sprinkle evenly with grated parmesan cheese.
11. Makes 8 servings.
For a more sophisticated look, shave the cheese with a vegetable peeler instead of grating it. If desired, garnish the platter with fresh orange sections.
